scope.note: 35 cases as described in scope + 2 morning-promoted GQL cases (GQL-048..GQL-055 = 8 from 1230 run, GQL-056..GQL-059 = 4 from 1700 run). Actual count: CHK-087..106=20, GQL-048..059=12, CUST-063+064=2, B2C-SHIP-001+002+014=3, B2C-ORG-004=1. Total=38 unique IDs reviewed.

G1_Structure.notes: All IDs unique, no parsing errors, 15-column format consistent. 3 High findings: BL-B2C-003/BL-B2C-004 do not exist in business-logic.md; CHK-106 Business_Rule empty for Critical/High case; GQL-055 missing postalCode in test step input (caused first execution skip).

G2_Determinism.notes: Overall tag usage is good. 4 findings: B2C-SHIP-001/002 use numbered-list steps without standard tag prefixes (legacy format inconsistency). CHK-106 precondition 'OQ-8 resolved' is non-deterministic. GQL-058 foreign memberId construction instruction is ambiguous ('alter last 2 digits'). B2C-ORG-004 step 4 WAIT is inline not a dedicated step line.

G3_Completeness.notes: CHK-106 missing Business_Rule (BL-B2B-001 applicable — duplicate-guard complements org address isolation). GQL-055 postalCode missing from test data (caused live skip). CUST-063/064 missing Cleanup='none' — empty rather than explicit.

G4_Testability.notes: GQL-057 cross-org override step is partly self-referential (it re-queries own org, then explains absence of override at a code-comment level rather than as a testable assertion). GQL-058 assertion 'isDuplicated field returns null' is acceptable only if treated as a pass condition — not a failure — see finding detail.

G5_DataValidity.notes: GQL-055: missing postalCode in Step input (postalCode is required in InputMemberAddressType per schema line 487). GQL-046 storeId schema mismatch (pre-existing defect, case was skipped in regression). CHK-050 uses countryCodes='US' but live schema confirms arg is 'countryCodes: String' not 'countryCode' — actual arg name verified correct. B2C-SHIP-001/002 use numbered steps which contain some hardcoded values (e.g. 'Jane, Doe, 456 New St') that are not bound via {{VAR}} — minor but technically violates no-hardcode rule for non-sensitive data.

G6_BLECLCoverage.notes: BL-B2C-003 and BL-B2C-004 referenced in B2C-SHIP-001, B2C-SHIP-002, B2C-ORG-004 do not exist in business-logic.md — these are assumed domain IDs. BL-CHK-001 is misapplied to address popup cases (it governs guest vs authenticated checkout, not address selection UI). PROPOSED-BL-PROFILE-001 on GQL-056 and B2C-SHIP-014 is tracked but not promoted — flagged as required follow-up after user approval.

G7_Duplication.notes: GQL-056 (API layer) and B2C-SHIP-014 (UI layer) cover same invariant at different layers — expected cross-layer pattern, not a duplicate. All CHK-* popup cases cover distinct scenarios (search, facets, empty state, sort, a11y, mobile, scope, compat, pagination, selection). No same-suite semantic duplicates detected.

G8_EnvVerification.notes: Verified via Phase 5 TLC-2026-04-23-1700 (5 targets, 0 console errors) and REG-2026-04-23-1700 execution (3 real bugs caught, multiple PASSes). No additional browser runs needed.

findings.issue: Business_Rule references BL-B2C-003 which does not exist in business-logic.md. The 'BL-B2C' domain is not defined. Likely should reference BL-B2B-001 (org address isolation) or be left blank.
findings.suggestedFix: Replace BL-B2C-003 with BL-B2B-001 (which covers org address scoping) or remove. Create BL-B2C-* domain entries if a new domain is warranted.

findings.issue: Business_Rule references BL-B2C-003 (same non-existent domain as B2C-SHIP-001). Additionally, test data has hardcoded personal values (Jane, Doe, 456 New St, Springfield, IL, 62701) not bound to {{VAR}} tokens.
findings.suggestedFix: Fix BL-B2C-003 reference. For hardcoded address data: acceptable to keep as literal seed values since they are non-sensitive and environment-independent; annotate in Preconditions that these are fixture values, not environment-dependent.

findings.issue: Business_Rule references BL-B2C-004 which does not exist in business-logic.md.
findings.suggestedFix: Replace BL-B2C-004 with BL-B2B-001 (org switching isolates cart, addresses, and lists — directly applicable).

findings.issue: Steps use numbered list format (1. [NAV]..., 2. [WAIT]...) instead of bare tag-prefixed lines. The test runner expects `[TAG] action` per line. Numbered format may cause parsing ambiguity.
findings.suggestedFix: Remove numbering — use bare `[NAV]`, `[WAIT]`, `[ASSERT]` prefixes. This is a legacy format issue from TLC-1230 sync.

findings.issue: Same numbered-list step format as B2C-SHIP-001. Also: step 3 explicitly names hardcoded address data ('Fill form: First Name: Jane, Last Name: Doe, Address: 456 New St...') — non-deterministic if test data changes.
findings.suggestedFix: Remove numbering. Hardcoded address data is acceptable as a fixture; add a comment that it is a fixed test address, not env-dependent.

findings.issue: Precondition states 'OQ-8 resolved to confirm duplicate-check UI is in scope'. This is a non-deterministic, human-approval gate embedded in an automated precondition. If OQ-8 is unresolved, the case cannot be executed and there is no fail-fast signal.
findings.suggestedFix: Replace OQ-8 gate with a concrete, observable precondition: 'checkDuplicateAddress query must be wired to the Add New address form in the popup (verify by inspecting network calls during address field entry).' Add ENV-004-style block: if checkDuplicateAddress is not called by UI, mark BLOCKED with reason.

findings.issue: Step for constructing FOREIGN_MEMBER_ID is ambiguous: 'alter the last 2 digits of <OWN_MEMBER_ID> OR use the TechFlow org member ID from seed data'. The 'alter last 2 digits' instruction does not guarantee a valid foreign memberId exists — an agent may produce an ID that simply returns 'member not found' rather than an authz bypass.
findings.suggestedFix: Hardcode the seed member ID from test-data or resolve it via a separate me { contact { id } } query with the second user slot. Prefer: 'Use John Mitchell memberId d0f765ba from agent pool slot 2 (from test-data/users/agent-user-pool.csv)' — this is the exact ID used in the regression run that caught BUG-050d-002.

findings.issue: Missing postalCode in test step input. InputMemberAddressType requires postalCode (per graphql-schema.md line 487: postalCode is required). The omission caused the case to be skipped on first attempt in REG-2026-04-23-1700 (execution note: 'failed due to missing postalCode in test data (test case defect)').
findings.suggestedFix: Add postalCode to the test address in the checkDuplicateAddress call: e.g. postalCode: \"00000\". Also add it to the Test_Data column binding.

findings.issue: Business_Rule column is empty for a High-priority case. BL-B2B-001 (org address isolation) and PROPOSED-BL-PROFILE-001 (server-side duplicate skip) are both applicable. PROPOSED-BL-PROFILE-001 is directly triggered by this case's scenario.
findings.suggestedFix: Add 'PROPOSED-BL-PROFILE-001' to Business_Rule column (acceptable as a proposed ID per project memory policy — not blocking but improves traceability).

findings.issue: Multiple CHK-* cases (CHK-087 through CHK-093, CHK-095 through CHK-098, CHK-101 through CHK-105) reference BL-CHK-001 for Business_Rule. BL-CHK-001 governs guest vs authenticated checkout, NOT address selection popup behavior. The invariant is technically correct (authenticated checkout must support address selection) but is a stretch. The address popup is a UI component governed more directly by BL-B2B-001 (org address scoping) or a to-be-created BL-ADDR-* rule.
findings.suggestedFix: Acceptable to keep BL-CHK-001 as a broad umbrella reference since no more specific BL exists for address popup. When PROPOSED-BL-PROFILE-001 is promoted, consider adding it to CHK-106 only. Low urgency — not blocking.

findings.issue: The 'cross-org override' step is descriptive prose ('since currentOrganizationAddresses has no orgId arg...') embedded in a step action. Steps should be executable actions, not documentation comments. The assertions correctly verify own-org count > 0, but there is no distinct step that would FAIL if cross-org override were somehow possible.
findings.suggestedFix: Move the explanatory prose into the References column. Keep the step as a direct action: '[ACT] Attempt any available query variant that could scope to a different org (orgId, memberId headers, etc.) — confirm schema has no such parameter.' The assertion then confirms schema-level absence of override vector.

findings.issue: PRE-EXISTING DEFECT (TLC-1230): GQL-046 uses storeId as an argument to currentCustomerAddresses. Per graphql-schema.md line 111, currentCustomerAddresses does NOT accept storeId. The case was SKIPPED in REG-2026-04-23-1700 with reason 'Unknown argument storeId'. This case is NOT in the VCST-4710 scope (it was promoted by TLC-1230), but it is in suite 050d and will cause a regression skip.
findings.suggestedFix: Remove storeId argument from GQL-046 query. Case is out-of-scope for this review but the defect should be tracked for the next lifecycle run on suite 050d.

findings.issue: postalCode missing from the checkDuplicateAddress input in the Steps column. Schema requires postalCode (NonNull on InputMemberAddressType). This was confirmed as a test case defect in regression execution — first run failed, re-test with corrected input passed.
findings.suggestedFix: Add postalCode: \"00000\" to the novel address input. Also update Test_Data column to bind postalCode.

findings.issue: Step uses countryCodes: \"US\" but the schema arg (graphql-schema.md line 111) is 'countryCodes: String' which takes comma-separated ISO-3 codes (e.g. 'USA'), not ISO-2 ('US'). The test ran via org user and returned totalCount=0, which could mask a wrong code value (no USA-coded addresses for that user).
findings.suggestedFix: Change 'US' to 'USA' (ISO-3) to match the seed data format (techflow-org-addresses-state-20260423.json uses countryCode='USA'). Verify live behavior.

findings.issue: Cleanup column appears empty (not 'none'). Minor — but policy requires explicit 'none' when there are no side effects.
Cleanup column appears empty (not 'none'). Same as CUST-063.

highValueCases.bugTitle: updateMemberAddresses does NOT silently skip duplicate addresses — inserts duplicate instead
checkDuplicateAddress allows cross-member authZ bypass — no error for foreign memberId
highValueCases.evidence: API response logged in test execution

highValueCases.bugTitle: Addresses page: duplicate address silently inserted instead of skipped
checkDuplicateAddress returns isDuplicated=false for addresses that DO exist in the address book
highValueCases.evidence: API response: isDuplicated=false for stored line1='300 QA Lane Updated', city='Chicago', countryCode='USA'
highValueCases.note: GQL-054 is from TLC-1230 scope (not TLC-1700 new). Included here because it caught a real bug in REG-1700.

readyWithKnownBlockers.reason: Column sort not wired — VCST-4992 open. Case correctly captures the known-broken state. PASS when defect fixed.
aria-label 'Search pickup locations' — VCST-4993 open. Case documents known defect.
Mobile 375px city facet clip — VCST-4994 open. Case documents known defect.
OQ-8 (duplicate-check UI wiring scope) unresolved. Precondition is non-deterministic. See finding GQL-106 D-003.

blockedNotCaseFault.reason: Multi-org user not in agent pool (ORG_USER_EMAIL = single-org). Test data gap, not case defect. Own-org baseline verified via GQL-057 PASS.
blockedNotCaseFault.resolution: Seed multi-org user in agent pool, set ORG_USER_EMAIL before re-run.

blockedNotCaseFault.reason: First attempt skipped due to missing postalCode (case defect — see finding). Re-test with corrected input PASSED. Mark as PASS after postalCode fix is applied to the case.
blockedNotCaseFault.resolution: Fix postalCode in Steps column (see G5 finding).

verdictRationale: No Blocker or Critical findings. 3 High-priority findings in G1/G2/G5 should be fixed soon but do not prevent regression execution. The 3 VCST-49xx-blocked cases have known open tickets and the case text correctly documents the blocked state. GQL-055 missing postalCode is a quick fix. BL-B2C-003/004 references are stale but do not affect executability. HIGH-VALUE cases already demonstrated in REG-1700 — catching 3 bugs and 1 security finding confirms the suite is effective.

recommendedFixes.changeDescription: Add postalCode: \"00000\" (or any 5-digit value) to the checkDuplicateAddress input in Steps column. This caused a skip in REG-1700 and will cause the same skip in regression if not fixed.

Replace ambiguous 'alter last 2 digits' foreign memberId construction with explicit seed data reference: use John Mitchell memberId d0f765ba from agent-user-pool.csv (confirmed to trigger BUG-050d-002 in REG-1700). Without this, the authZ bypass test is unreliable.
Remove numbered list format from Steps column. Replace '1. [NAV]' with bare '[NAV]', etc. Template compliance.
Same as B2C-SHIP-001: remove numbered list format. Also fix Business_Rule BL-B2C-003 → BL-B2B-001.
Change countryCodes filter value from 'US' to 'USA' (ISO-3 format per seed data). Low risk but prevents false empty results.
Replace OQ-8 gate in Preconditions with concrete observable precondition. Add PROPOSED-BL-PROFILE-001 to Business_Rule column.
Remove storeId argument from currentCustomerAddresses query (pre-existing defect outside VCST-4710 scope). Prevents recurring skip in suite 050d.
